goal: Queue 6 cameras x 1 frame 4K path-traced with AOVs for comp, rendered overnight headless.
thoughts: Path-traced 4K x 6 frames demands the kit headless batch runner, not GUI capture. Denoiser stays ON for beauty only; OFF for normals/Z/diffuse/specular/cryptomatte because a temporally denoised utility pass breaks comp. Be honest: RTX Interactive's cryptomatte emission is limited -- verify the real AOV list before promising channel names.
code:
set_render_resolution(width=3840, height=2160)
set_render_mode(mode="RTXInteractivePathTraced")
available = lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim 5.x RTX renderer AOV inventory cryptomatte")
set_render_config(
    samples_per_pixel="<SPP>",
    max_bounces="<INT>",
    denoiser="optix",
    denoise_aovs=False,
    aovs=["beauty", "diffuse", "specular", "normal", "depth", "object_id"]
)
for cam in ["cam_01", "cam_02", "cam_03", "cam_04", "cam_05", "cam_06"]:
    capture_viewport(
        path=f"<OUT_DIR>/{cam}.exr",
        camera_path=f"/World/Cameras/{cam}",
        width=3840, height=2160,
        file_format="exr_multilayer"
    )
# Wrap above in kit headless script: isaac-sim.headless.sh --exec render_batch.py
failure_modes: Fabricating cryptomatte AOV support without checking the actual renderer
Leaving denoiser on for normals/Z so compositor gets unusable passes
Suggesting GUI 'press capture 6 times' instead of headless kit runner
